LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO MARCH 6, 2012 AT 9:30AM TO ENABLE PLAINTIFF TO COMPLETE DISCOVERY. ALL DEPOSITIONS ARE TO BE COMPLETED BY FEBRUARY 1, 2012. ANY SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION PAPERS SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY PERSONAL SERVICE OR EMAIL BY FEBRUARY 8, 2012. ANY SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY PAPERS SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY PERSONAL SERVICE OR EMAIL BY FEBRUARY 24, 2012. COURTESY COPIES SHALL BE PROVIDED TO DEPARTMENT 302. FURTHER, THE COURT INDICATED THAT DISCOVERY REMAINS OPEN ONLY AS TO THE DEPOSITIONS THAT WERE SOUGHT IN ORDER TO OPPOSE THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. IN ADDITION, THE COURT CONTINUED THE TRIAL DATE TO APRIL 16, 2012 (PARTIES WAIVE NOTICE AS TO THE TRIAL DATE), AND VACATED THE MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE CURRENTLY SET FOR FEBRUARY 10, 2012. PLAINTIFF IS TO FILE A MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO BE HEARD ON MARCH 6, 2012. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

No other Appearance. The Tentative Ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF SARAH LARSON Motion To Tax Costs. Granted as to the claimed amounts for database creation and denied as to the claimed amounts for copies of summary judgment exhibits ($138) and delivery fees. The delivery costs and copying of summary judgment exhibits were reasonably and necessarily incurred. The database was not necessary and is a substitute for non-recoverable paralegal fees. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2012 Continued To Mar-06-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Rafal Ofierski of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4244) for defendant and moving party; Mary Shea Hagebols of the Shea Law Offices (510.208.4422) for plaintiff and responding party.
Discussed and the court modified its ruling as follows: continued to March 6, 2012 at 9:30am to enable Plaintiff to complete discovery. All depositions are to be completed by February 1, 2012. Any supplemental opposition papers shall be filed and served by personal service or email by February 8, 2012. Any supplemental reply papers shall be filed and served by personal service or email by February 24, 2012. Courtesy copies shall be provided to Department 302. Further, the court indicated that discovery remains open only as to the depositions that were sought in order to oppose the motion for summary judgment. In addition, the court continued the trial date to April 16, 2012 (parties waive notice as to the trial date), and vacated the mandatory settlement conference currently set for February 10, 2012. Plaintiff is to file a motion to amend the complaint to be heard on March 6, 2012. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
